Docker configs, nginx rewrite rules, and tests to match the actual
production deployment prefix.
- Moves `VITE_BASE_ROUTE` out of the base `docker-compose.yml` into the
ingress-sim overlay, so the default local setup serves from root (`/`)
without a subpath.

commit.message:
feat: per-environment platform selection (--claude/--copilot)

Each environment now targets exactly one platform:
- skenv create myenv (default: --claude -> ~/.claude/skills/)
- skenv create myenv --copilot (-> ~/.copilot/skills/)
- Platform stored in .platform file, locked at creation
- One env per platform can be active simultaneously
- Separate .active-claude / .active-copilot tracking files
- Per-platform _pre-skenv backups (_pre-skenv-claude, _pre-skenv-copilot)
- Cross-platform inheritance rejected, same-platform enforced
- Shell prompt shows both: [skenv:claude:myenv copilot:other]
- Freeze manifests include platform, init reads it
- cmd_run saves/restores per-platform state
- Base layer remains universal (synced to whichever platform is active)
- Updated README with platform docs, new architecture diagram, FAQ

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(makefile): add .env-aware Docker workflow

- setup: create .env from .env.example (skip if exists)
- check-env: validate required vars (LLM_BASE_URL, LLM_MODEL_ID,
  PROMETHEUS_URL) before run/release
- run: pass --env-file .env to docker run; supports EXTRA for

fix: auto-detect URL in `c8 load plugin` to prevent broken registry state (#120)

* Initial plan

* fix: auto-detect URL in c8 load plugin to prevent broken registry state

When a URL is passed as a positional argument to `c8 load plugin` without
the `--from` flag, the command now auto-detects it and routes it through
the URL install path. This correctly extracts the package name from
`package.json` instead of storing the URL as the plugin name in the
registry.

- Added `isUrl()` helper that detects https://, http://, git://, file://, and shorthand protocols
- Auto-route URL positional args to the `fromUrl` path in the load plugin handler
- `--from` is now optional for URLs (backwards compatible)
- Updated help text to show URLs can be passed directly

Co-authored-by: jan-ladleif <17142708+jan-ladleif@users.noreply.github.com>

* Work on review comments; make --from mandatory again for URLs; move input val downwards

* Fix file protocol being allowed with one slash

* Add : back to protocol names

---------

commit.message:
Make hooks the ONLY DB writer — remove agent manual INSERT

Root cause: agents manually INSERT into tasks table with guessed
project_id, causing misattribution. Hooks have a deterministic
registration gate (cwd → folder match) but agents don't.

Fix:
- Removed 'insert a row into tasks table' from copilot-instructions.md
  and CLAUDE.md. Agents never touch progress.db directly.
- Rewrote progress-logger.sh to read Copilot CLI session state:
  workspace.yaml (summary) + checkpoints (overview, takeaways, gotchas)
  instead of just file diffs.
- Falls back to activity log file changes if no session state found.
- Skips index.md when finding latest checkpoint.

The hook pipeline is now the single deterministic write path:
  sessionEnd → match cwd to project → read session state → INSERT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ai-progress-tracker
repository.description: Tool-agnostic progress tracking for AI-assisted development

commit.message:
doc: add node-requirements.md -- formal node firmware requirements (#11)

* doc: add node-requirements.md -- formal node firmware requirements

New file docs/node-requirements.md with 33 requirements (ND-0100
through ND-0702) covering:

- Protocol and communication (frame format, CBOR encoding, frame size)
- Wake cycle (WAKE message, COMMAND processing, sleep interval)
- Authentication and replay protection (HMAC, response binding,
  sequence numbers, nonce generation)
- Key storage and provisioning (PSK flash partition, USB pairing,
  factory reset, secure boot)
- Program transfer and execution (chunked transfer, hash verification,
  A/B partitions, ephemeral storage, post-update execution)
- BPF environment (helper API stability, bus/communication/map/system
  helpers, execution constraints, map memory budget)
- Timing and retries (WAKE retry, chunk retry, response timeout)

Also adds node-requirements.md to README Further Reading section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR review on node-requirements.md

- Fix ND-0103 source reference: protocol.md section 3.3 not 3.2
- Fix ND-0103 acceptance criteria: constrain blob size based on
  fully encoded frame size, not raw payload budget
- Fix ND-0203: clarify set_next_wake() is min(requested, base) and
  cannot extend or modify the base interval
- Fix ND-0605: verifier runs on gateway (Prevail), not on node;
  node firmware enforces stack/call-depth limits at runtime

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR review round 2 on node-requirements.md

- Fix ND-0200: allow multiple WAKE attempts (retries) per wake cycle;
  at most one COMMAND exchange
- Fix ND-0304: replace subjective 'statistically independent' with
  testable criteria (no repeats over 1000 cycles, RNG health check)
- Fix ND-0602: send() must not wait for APP_DATA_REPLY but may block
  briefly for transmission
- Fix ND-0702: concrete timeout criteria (50ms on ESP-NOW, measured
  from frame transmission, transport-defined for others)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR review round 3 on node-requirements.md

- Fix ND-0702: change SHALL to MUST for consistency with MoSCoW style
- Fix ND-0604: add missing delay_us() and bpf_trace_printk() helpers;
  reference bpf-environment.md for complete API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: A programmable, verifiable runtime for distributed sensor nodes. Nodes run uniform firmware and execute behavior defined by uBPF programs verified with Prevail. commit.message:
feat: ShardCore, StateRegistry, message enums, and mutation logic (#3)

* feat: ShardCore, StateRegistry, message enums, and mutation logic

PR 2 of the dev plan — implements the pure state machine core:

- ShardCore<S, V>: pure state machine with ArcSwap view map
  - apply_mutation / apply_mutation_with_delta
  - accept_inbound_snapshot / accept_inbound_delta
  - should_accept (incarnation, age) ordering
  - stale_peers, mark_stale, on_node_joined, on_node_left
- StateRegistry: type-erased shard storage with AnyStateShard trait
  - register, lookup, lookup_typed, broadcast_node_joined/left
- Message enums: SimpleShardMsg, DeltaShardMsg, SyncEngineMsg, ChangeFeedMsg
- InboundSnapshot struct for clean parameter passing

Tests: 43 ShardCore unit tests + 9 registry integration tests
Coverage: TEST-05..07, PVM-01..03, SHARD-01..08,10, MUT-01..06, DMUT-01..06,
         REG-01..07, REG-04a, plus additional edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace monolithic ArcSwap view map with per-node ArcSwap

Introduces ViewMap<V> — a two-level ArcSwap data structure that avoids
cloning the entire HashMap on every single-node update:

  ArcSwap<HashMap<NodeId, Arc<ArcSwap<StateViewObject<V>>>>>
    outer (rare: node join/leave)    inner (frequent: O(1) atomic swap)

Performance characteristics:
- Read single node: 2 atomic loads, fully lock-free
- Update single node: 1 atomic store, no map clone (was O(n) deep clone)
- Node join/leave: clone outer map (cheap Arc pointer bumps), rare
- Snapshot all: O(n) — unchanged, but now only needed for bulk reads

ShardCore no longer exposes the raw view_map. New APIs:
- get_view(NodeId) -> Option<Arc<StateViewObject<V>>>
- snapshot() -> HashMap<NodeId, StateViewObject<V>>
- view_count() -> usize

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: introduce StateVersion struct, simplify delta API

- Add StateVersion { incarnation, age } with derived Ord (lexicographic)
  replacing scattered (incarnation, age) pairs throughout the codebase
- StateObject/StateViewObject now use version: StateVersion instead of
  separate age/incarnation fields
- Merge pending_remote_age + pending_remote_incarnation into single
  pending_remote_version: Option<StateVersion>
- Remove to_age from accept_inbound_delta — delta always advances by 1
- Remove ShardCore::should_accept() — replaced by StateVersion::cmp()
- Simplify InboundSnapshot, MutationOutcome, DeltaMutationOutcome to
  carry version: StateVersion instead of separate fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename StateVersion to Generation

Avoids confusion with wire_version and storage_version fields.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename version field to generation

Renames the 'version' field in StateObject, StateViewObject, and
InboundSnapshot to 'generation' to avoid any confusion with
wire_version and storage_version fields. Also renames
pending_remote_version to pending_remote_generation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use Generation throughout all message enums and simplify delta API

- Replace incarnation/age fields with Generation in shard_msg, sync_msg, feed_msg, sync_message
- Remove from_age/to_age from delta messages; use single generation field
- Remove min_required_age from RequestSnapshot (YAGNI)
- Fix gap detection test to use correct generation values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update design docs for Generation, ViewMap, and simplified delta API

- design.md: §2.3 envelopes use Generation, §3.1 two-level ArcSwap ViewMap,
  §5.1 Generation struct with Ord, §6.2 updated messages/workflows,
  §12 wire protocol uses Generation
- design-history.md: add Iteration 35 (ViewMap, Generation, delta simplification)
- dev-plan.md: update PR 2 status and description
- test-plan.md: update test descriptions for Generation terminology

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review findings from GPT-5.4 and Gemini

- Fix pending_remote_generation clearing: only clear when accepted
  generation >= pending (was blindly clearing on every accept)
- Fix node join placeholder: mark as stale via pending_remote_generation
  so query path triggers pull for real state
- Fix StateRegistry::register type safety: take S: AnyStateShard directly
  instead of Box<dyn AnyStateShard> with separate type param (prevents
  TypeId/value mismatch)
- Document ViewMap single-writer safety model (actor-serialized writes,
  load-then-store is safe with single writer)
- Add 3 new tests: partial catch-up preserves pending (snapshot + delta),
  node join placeholder marked stale

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Yaming Liu (MWT) <ymliu@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.1.1-beta.2

- Device picker uses native HA searchable selector with entity domain
  filtering (sensor, binary_sensor, switch)

cfl: CFL-004 ToneMapFunc HBO fix + SafeDescribe alignment for all 6 fuzzers

CFL-004: CIccToneMapFunc::Read() validates m_nParameters >= NumArgs()
before allocating m_params. Prevents HBO when Describe() accesses
m_params[0..2] with only 1 float allocated (CWE-122).

SafeDescribe pattern: All 6 Describe-calling fuzzers now use
CflSafeDescribe.h which runs Validate() before Describe(). Tags with
icValidateCriticalError are skipped, preventing crashes from partially
loaded internal state (alignment issue: fuzzers call Describe()
unconditionally, upstream tools only in specific modes).

Affected fuzzers: dump, deep_dump, profile, calculator, spectral, tiffdump.
Verified: crash-6ec5f76cd5d6c934c111eb59bc81ea44362ca3ee exits 0 on all 6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Route accumulator by state_class, not device_class

Only use the energy accumulator for sensors with state_class:
total_increasing and a supported energy unit (kWh/Wh/MWh). All other
sensors, including energy sensors without total_increasing, are plain
mirrors. This fixes double-counting on devices that report cumulative
energy (summation_delivered) rather than instantaneous power.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[code-simplifier] Simplify URL normalization logic in CDN utilities (#18062)

## Code Simplification - 2026-03-10

This PR simplifies recently modified code to improve clarity,
consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality.

### Files Simplified

- `packages/dev/core/src/Misc/tools.ts` - Simplified URL normalization
in `GetAssetUrl` and `GetBabylonScriptURL` methods
- `packages/tools/ktx2Decoder/src/transcoder.ts` - Simplified URL
normalization in `GetWasmUrl` method

### Improvements Made

1. **Enhanced Clarity**
- Replaced verbose `str[str.length - 1] === "/"` checks with cleaner
`str.endsWith("/")`
- Replaced `substring(0, str.length - 1)` with more concise `slice(0,
-1)`
- Used template literals instead of string concatenation for versioned
URL construction

2. **Improved Consistency**
   - Applied the same pattern across all three URL normalization methods
- Made the code more maintainable with a consistent approach to trailing
slash removal

3. **Preserved Functionality**
   - All changes are purely stylistic - no behavioral changes
   - Logic flow remains identical
   - Same error handling and edge cases

### Changes Based On

Recent changes from:
- #18047 - Add support for versioned CDN URLs in Tools and Transcoder
classes
- Commit 793d073 - Add support for versioned CDN URLs

### Code Examples

**Before:**
````typescript
const baseUrl = Tools.ScriptBaseUrl[Tools.ScriptBaseUrl.length - 1] === "/" 
    ? Tools.ScriptBaseUrl.substring(0, Tools.ScriptBaseUrl.length - 1) 
    : Tools.ScriptBaseUrl;
const versionedBase = Tools._DefaultCdnUrl + "/v" + Tools._CdnVersion;
````

**After:**
````typescript
const baseUrl = Tools.ScriptBaseUrl.endsWith("/") 
    ? Tools.ScriptBaseUrl.slice(0, -1) 
    : Tools.ScriptBaseUrl;
const versionedBase = `\$\{Tools._DefaultCdnUrl}/v\$\{Tools._CdnVersion}`;
````

### Testing

- ✅ TypeScript syntax validated
- ✅ No functional changes - behavior is identical
- ✅ Simplified code is more readable and maintainable

### Review Focus

Please verify:
- Functionality is preserved (string operations produce identical
results)
- Simplifications improve code quality
- Changes align with project conventions
- No unintended side effects

---

*Automated by Code Simplifier Agent - analyzing code from the last 24
hours*

commit.message:
Fix multi-user 'interaction failed' with defer + per-session lock

All ScoreboardView and FoulSelectView button/select callbacks now:
1. Call interaction.response.defer() immediately to acknowledge Discord
   within the 3-second window regardless of processing time
2. Acquire the per-session asyncio.Lock (added to SnookerSession._lock)
   to serialize concurrent state mutations from multiple users
3. Call interaction.edit_original_response() after the lock is released

This eliminates race conditions and timeout failures when two users
operate the bot simultaneously from separate devices.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(daemon): stop ignoring cancel/load results, add global error middleware, harden Timeouts

- Part 1: Cancel and load-script endpoints now check result strings for
  errors and return 500 with error details instead of always reporting
  success. Follows existing hardReset pattern.

- Part 2: Add errorHandlingMiddleware as ASP.NET pipeline middleware so
  ALL routes get exception handling automatically. Previously 5 of 32
  endpoints lacked withErrorHandling wrappers. Remove per-endpoint
  withErrorHandling calls since middleware now covers everything. SSE
  streams are handled safely via Response.HasStarted check.

- Part 3: Add ValidTimeout single-case DU with create/value for
  validated timeout ranges (1s-10min). Make mutable test timeouts
  thread-safe with locks. setPerTestTimeout and setGlobalTestRunTimeout
  now validate via ValidTimeout before accepting.

- Part 4: Add env var overrides for top 5 timeouts:
  SAGEFS_WARMUP_INACTIVITY_SECONDS, SAGEFS_PER_TEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
  SAGEFS_BUILD_TIMEOUT_MINUTES, SAGEFS_WARMUP_MAX_MINUTES,
  SAGEFS_WORKER_HTTP_READ_SECONDS. Read once at module init with
  envOrDefault/envOrDefaultMinutes helpers.

- Part 5: Add TimeoutsTests covering ValidTimeout validation, thread-safe
  setter rejection/acceptance, env var override positivity checks, and
  static timeout value assertions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix DblclickZoomInteraction not disabling double click zoom (#113) (#120)

.NET's camelCase JSON serializer converts DblclickZoomInteraction to
dblclickZoomInteraction (lowercase 'c' in click), but the Azure Maps SDK
expects dblClickZoomInteraction (capital 'C' in Click). The property was
silently ignored, so double click zoom was never disabled.

Fix by adding [JsonPropertyName("dblClickZoomInteraction")] to ensure
the correct key is sent to the SDK.

Fixes #113

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth -> clone-deep -> is-plain-object@5 is
ESM-only and crashes in Vercel's CJS runtime. On Vercel, use plain
playwright-core directly (no addExtra/stealth) to avoid the broken
dep chain. Stealth plugin still used locally.

commit.message:
fix: address PR review — PORT validation, pure resolveConfig, no process.exit

- Move PORT env var reading from resolveConfig() to startServer() to keep
  config resolution pure and deterministic for library consumers
- Add PORT validation: must be finite integer 1-65535, warns on invalid
- Replace process.exit(1) with reject(err) so library consumers can
  catch/handle EADDRINUSE instead of being killed
- Improve error message: generic config reference instead of hardcoded
  'src/server.ts', concrete PORT=3001 example instead of port+1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

commit.message:
Add undo support in full mode (snapshot stack)

- SetState._undo_stack stores up to 20 snapshots of mutable state
  (scores, current_break, breaks, events, current_player_idx)
- _save_snapshot() called before add_score(), apply_foul(), next_player()
- undo() pops and restores the last snapshot; can_undo() for UI state
- UndoButton added to ScoreboardView row 2 (disabled when nothing to undo)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cfl: add CFL-004 patch for UBSAN enum load in CIccCalculatorFunc::Read()

Read32() into icChannelFuncSignature enum with invalid file data
(e.g. 0xC143C5E4) is undefined behavior per C++17 §[dcl.enum]/8.
The value is rejected by the sig!=GetType() comparison, but the UB
fires on the load before the comparison executes.

Fix: read into icUInt32Number, cast GetType() for comparison.

File: IccMpeCalc.cpp:3477-3482
CWE-843 (Access of Resource Using Incompatible Type)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Prometheus intelligent anomaly analyzer

- Capability probe to auto-detect LLM tool-calling support
  and select the best analysis mode (A/B/C)
- Mode A: Full Agentic - LLM autonomously explores metrics
  via multi-turn function calling
- Mode B: Semi Auto - pre-scan anomalies with z-score,
  LLM does guided single-round deep dive
- Mode C: Static - all data pre-collected, LLM does
  one-shot root cause analysis
- Prometheus HTTP API client with range query, metadata,
  and label enumeration
- Z-score based anomaly detector scans all metrics
  and returns ranked anomaly report
- OpenAI-compatible API integration (cline/coder-flash)
- Multi-stage Dockerfile with non-root user
- Makefile for git-commit-tagged image build and push
  to hwchiu/mohw container registry

docs: add comprehensive developer guide

Create consolidated docs/developer-guide.md covering:
- Quick start (setup, tests, quality gates)
- Architecture overview (layers, patterns, source tree)
- Adding a new line of business (6-step walkthrough)
- Product configuration (YAML schema, coverage tables)
- Rating engine (7 factor tables, formula, calibration)
- Agent development (base class, Foundry integration, orchestration)
- Knowledge graph (YAML structure, seeding, retrieval)

- Deployment (local, Bicep IaC, containers, Foundry agents, CI/CD)

- Coding standards (Python, TypeScript, testing, security)

Closes #37

commit.message:
Update R/update_readme_log.R

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Multi-layer model with spectral norm clamping

- model.py: Support num_layers distinct W matrices, applied sequentially.
  Orthogonal init (spectral norm 1.0), ReLU provides natural contraction.
- train.py: Spectral norm clamping after each optimizer step prevents
  instability. Added --num-layers and --device args.
- generate.py: NPU path makes num_layers calls per character step,
  swapping W between calls. Added --depth override for inference.
- README.md: Added Phase 4 char LM section with usage and results.

8-layer model (148K params) reaches val loss 3.17 vs 1-layer 3.47.
NPU throughput: 287K-342K total chars/s (1152 parallel sequences).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update R/update_readme_log.R

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
